title: story-22-review-churn
description:
This is a continuation of the broader review-churn pattern, now centered on story 22. In the current window, activity resumed with a successful dev run ending at 2026-05-29T01:27:10.347304+00:00, followed immediately by a successful reviewer run ending at 2026-05-29T01:27:28.362692+00:00. The flagged watcher note reports that story 22 is now at 7 cumulative reviewer cycles and is active again in this window, which is well beyond the expected convergence ceiling.

There is no failure signal here: both runs succeeded, and the note explicitly reports no failed runs, retry clustering, spend spike, tick activity, queue skew, or prompt-placeholder leak in scope. The concern is structural rather than error-driven: the factory is continuing to spend on repeated dev->reviewer loops without evidence of the story advancing out of review churn. The latest dev run alone consumed 254.542 seconds and $0.614868, so the pattern is producing non-trivial ongoing cost even in a window with otherwise normal infrastructure signals.

Possible explanations remain limited to non-convergence in the review loop rather than a runtime fault. Given the repeated successful cycles and absence of infrastructure errors, the most plausible area is prompt or dispatch behavior governing how review feedback is carried forward between personas. The evidence does not support a stronger claim than sustained active churn.

evidence.summary_excerpt: Activity resumed on story 22 with a successful dev run followed immediately by a successful reviewer run, bringing it to 7 cumulative reviewer cycles and making its review churn active again in this window.

evidence.excerpt: {"noteworthy":"Story 22 is active in this window and now at 7 reviewer cycles, which exceeds the escalation threshold for active review churn."}
{"noteworthy":"Worktree drift persists without growth: story 33 is still active in reviewer_requested_changes, and stories 52 and 59 remain missing from the DB."}

escalation_reason: Active review-churn pattern on story 22 has persisted into this interval and is now at 7 reviewer cycles, with another successful dev->reviewer loop completed by 2026-05-29T01:27:28.362692+00:00. L3 judgment is needed because this is sustained non-convergence with ongoing cost burn ($0.614868 dev run plus $0.0464435 reviewer run in this window) despite no infrastructure failure signal.
